creator: ohauer@gmx.de
text:
Hi,

a former colleague asked me some days ago if I have time to look into this port ...

Seems Rene was faster, but there are still some issues with the port.

I also shaped a patch that rewrites the port to use new port framework parts.
- add entry to ports/(G|U)IDs
- instead using entries twice in pkg-plist %%(AGENT|SERVER)%%/same/file/for/both use
  %%SERVER_AGENT%% (similar to NXMAP)
- move DB backends into a OPTION_GROUP to separate them in the config dialog
- sort pkg-plist


Anyway, the ports builds fine with all kind of different options on FreeBSD 8.4,
but on 9.2 and on 10 it seems the following files are not build.

From `make package' on a 9.2 and 10 system:
 pkg-static: lstat(/usr/ports/net-mgmt/netxms/work/stage/usr/local/lib/libnsm_netsvc.so): No such file or directory
 pkg-static: lstat(/usr/ports/net-mgmt/netxms/work/stage/usr/local/lib/netxms/netsvc.nsm): No such file or directory

The rewritten port can be found here:
http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/netxms-1.2.12.diff

Unless we have sub-ports, perhaps think about a dedicated netxms-client port (like postgresql$number-(client|server)

One last question:
Is it really required to `chown netxms:netxms ${DATADIR}' ?
This looks not right to me.


-- 
olli
